Package: `prompting-press`. Version: `latest`.

## Prompt

### `Prompt`

```ts
class Prompt
```

An immutable, fully-validated prompt. Wraps a `NapiPrompt` handle; all construction invariants (shape-valid, template-parseable, agreement-sound, reserved-name clean) are enforced by the Rust consumer at construction time. There are no setters; the sole mutator is Prompt.derive (T045). ## Construction â€” four entry points, all throwing on invalid input ```ts const p = new Prompt(shape, validators?); const p = Prompt.fromYaml(text, validators?); const p = Prompt.fromJson(text, validators?); const p = Prompt.fromToml(text, validators?); // TOML routed to Rust, no smol-toml dep ``` ## validators? â€” validation_required coverage check When supplied, any variable with `validation_required: true` must appear in `validators.shape`. Construction throws a PromptValidationError if a required variable is uncovered. When `validators.shape` is absent (non-`ZodObject` schema), the check is skipped. ## render() â€” validate-then-render ```ts p.render(schema, data, opts?); // schema form: safeParse before templating p.render(data, opts?); // static form (or uses bound validators when present) ``` ## derive(overlay, validators?) â€” sole mutator Shallow-replaces top-level fields; re-validates the merged whole. Validators carry forward from the source by default; pass `validators` to override.

#### `body`

```ts
get body(): string
```

The root body template source (the default arm's unrendered template text).

#### `metadata`

```ts
get metadata(): Record<string, unknown>
```

The `metadata` opaque map (library-defined top-level annotations, if any).

#### `name`

```ts
get name(): string
```

The prompt's name (the `name` field of the underlying definition).

#### `outputModel`

```ts
get outputModel(): string | undefined
```

The `output_model` reference, if declared. Carried as metadata only â€” the library never parses against it.

#### `role`

```ts
get role(): string
```

The conversational role (`"system"` / `"user"` / `"assistant"`).

#### `variables`

```ts
get variables(): object | undefined
```

The declared variables map (`&#123; [name]: PromptVariable &#125;`). Read-only metadata. Each entry carries the variable's `type`, `origin`, and optional `validation_required`.

#### `variants`

```ts
get variants(): object | undefined
```

The named variants map (`&#123; [name]: PromptVariant &#125;`). Empty object when the prompt has no named variants (only the implicit `default` arm).

#### `constructor`

```ts
new Prompt(shape: PromptDefinition, validators?: ValidatorMap): Prompt
```

Primary public constructor â€” constructs a `Prompt` from a `PromptDefinition`-shaped object. The optional third parameter `_internal` is a module-private token (type `InternalCtorArg`) that can only be formed by code within this module. External callers cannot construct a valid `InternalCtorArg` value (its type uses a `unique symbol` brand), so `new Prompt(shape, v?)` is effectively the only publicly-callable form. The static factories (`fromYaml`, `fromJson`, `fromToml`) and `with()` use the internal path to avoid re-running the Rust validator on an already-validated handle.

#### `check`

```ts
check(): CheckReport
```

Pure advisory lint: returns a CheckReport containing only the origin/guard finding class (`"untrusted_without_guard"`). Construction already enforces agreement, parse, and reserved-name invariants; those are structurally unreachable here. Pure: never renders, never mutates.

#### `derive`

```ts
derive(overlay: Partial<PromptDefinition>, validators?: ValidatorMap): Prompt
```

The sole mutator: shallow-replace top-level fields from `overlay` onto a clone of this prompt's definition, then re-validate the merged whole via the Rust consumer. The original `Prompt` is untouched. Validators carry forward from the source by default; pass `validators` to override/augment. Coverage is re-checked against the merged definition.

#### `fromJson`

```ts
static fromJson(text: string, validators?: ValidatorMap): Prompt
```

Construct a `Prompt` from already-read **JSON** text.

#### `fromToml`

```ts
static fromToml(text: string, validators?: ValidatorMap): Prompt
```

Construct a `Prompt` from already-read **TOML** text. TOML parsing is done by the Rust consumer (`toml@1.1.2` via `Prompt::from_toml`). Raw text is routed to the addon â€” no `smol-toml` or other JS TOML library needed.

#### `fromYaml`

```ts
static fromYaml(text: string, validators?: ValidatorMap): Prompt
```

Construct a `Prompt` from already-read **YAML** text. The text is routed to the Rust consumer's `Prompt::from_yaml` â€” no JS YAML parsing. Error semantics mirror `new Prompt()`.

#### `getSource`

```ts
getSource(opts?: { variant: string } | null): string
```

Return a variant's **unrendered** template source (the exact string the kernel hashes into `templateHash`). Pure: no vars, no validation. `opts.variant` selects an arm (absent â‡’ the reserved `default`).

#### `render`

```ts
render(data: unknown, opts?: RenderOptions | null): RenderResult
```

Render this prompt's resolved variant with typed inputs, validating first. Three call forms, selected at runtime: - **Schema form:** `render(schema, data, opts?)` â€” `schema.safeParse(data)` runs here, before any templating; on failure a PromptValidationError is thrown and the kernel is **never reached**. - **Static form:** `render(data, opts?)` â€” already-typed plain data, marshaled directly (no Zod check at render time). - **Bound-validator form:** `render(data, opts?)` when the prompt was constructed with `validators` â€” the bound schema's `safeParse(data)` runs automatically. `opts` carries `&#123; variant?, guard? &#125;`.

## RenderResult

### `RenderResult`

```ts
class RenderResult
```

A rendered prompt + its content-addressed provenance, read-only from JS. The Node mirror of the kernel's RenderResult (; ). Surfaced **1:1** â€” the binding adds nothing and interprets nothing. A Read-only class with camelCase JS accessors (`template_hash` â†’ `templateHash`). A result is produced by [`render`], never constructed from JS.

#### `guard`

```ts
get guard(): string | null
```

The opt-in guard instruction text (present iff a guard was enabled and the prompt declares an untrusted/external field); `null` for a plain render. Never part of `text`.

#### `name`

```ts
get name(): string
```

The prompt name.

#### `renderHash`

```ts
get renderHash(): string
```

Lowercase-hex `SHA256(rendered text)`. Surfaces as `renderHash`.

#### `templateHash`

```ts
get templateHash(): string
```

Lowercase-hex `SHA256(resolved variant source)`. Surfaces as `templateHash`.

#### `text`

```ts
get text(): string
```

The rendered body text. The guard text is NEVER concatenated here.

#### `variant`

```ts
get variant(): string
```

The resolved variant name (the reserved `default`, or the named arm).

## GuardConfig

### `GuardConfig`

```ts
interface GuardConfig
```

The opt-in guard-expansion config, accepted from JS as a plain object and **plumbed through** to the kernel. A 1:1 mirror of the kernel's GuardConfig â€” `enabled` plus an optional override `template`. This is **config only**; it carries no logic. The kernel owns guard *expansion*; the binding only marshals these two fields across the boundary and surfaces whatever [`RenderResult::guard`] the kernel populates. Accepted from JS as a plain TS object `&#123; enabled, template? &#125;`.

#### `enabled`

```ts
enabled: boolean
```

When `false`, the render is plain and [`RenderResult::guard`] is `None`.

#### `template`

```ts
template?: string
```

Optional caller override of the guard instruction text; absent â‡’ the kernel default.

### `RenderOptions`

```ts
interface RenderOptions
```

Options for Prompt.render.

#### `guard`

```ts
guard?: GuardConfig | null
```

Opt-in guard config; absent / `&#123; enabled: false &#125;` â‡’ a plain render (`RenderResult.guard === null`).

#### `unsafeRevealRenderDetail`

```ts
unsafeRevealRenderDetail?: boolean
```

**Off by default.** When `true`, the full underlying render-error detail is surfaced in the thrown PromptRenderError``.errors[0].message` instead of the fixed scrubbed string. **Risk:** enabling this may place **bound-value content** â€” untrusted input, PII, secrets â€” into the thrown error and into any log line or stack trace derived from it. Use only in a controlled debug context with a trusted log destination, and only after deliberately accepting that exposure. Never set `true` by default or via ambient configuration.

#### `variant`

```ts
variant?: string
```

Select a named variant arm; absent â‡’ the reserved `default` arm.

## CheckReport

### `CheckReport`

```ts
class CheckReport
```

The output of [`check`]: an ordered, read-only list of [`Finding`]s. Empty â‡’ the lint passed. The Node mirror of the consumer's CheckReport (; ). Surfaced **1:1** â€” the binding adds nothing and interprets nothing. Read-only class with read-only accessors; a report is produced by [`check`], never constructed from JS.

#### `findings`

```ts
get findings(): Finding[]
```

Every lint failure found, in the consumer's deterministic order. Empty â‡’ pass.

#### `isEmpty`

```ts
isEmpty(): boolean
```

`report.isEmpty()` â€” alias for [`passed`](Self::passed): `true` iff there are no findings.

#### `passed`

```ts
passed(): boolean
```

`report.passed()` â€” `true` iff there are no findings (the lint passed). Reads clearly at a CI gate (`if (!check(reg).passed()) process.exit(1)`).

## Finding

### `Finding`

```ts
interface Finding
```

One actionable lint finding, read-only from JS. The Node mirror of the consumer's Finding. It names the `prompt`, the `variant` where applicable (`null` for a prompt-level provenance finding), the failure `kind` (a stable snake_case discriminant string â€” see the module docs), and a human-readable `detail`. The `detail` carries no bound-value content. Surfaced as a plain JS object with all four fields (the natural shape for the `findings` array the TS facade iterates / matches on).

#### `detail`

```ts
detail: string
```

A human-readable, actionable description. Carries no bound-value content.

#### `kind`

```ts
kind: string
```

The failure kind as a stable snake_case **discriminant string** â€” the value JS matches on. Currently always `"untrusted_without_guard"`. The kind's inner datum is echoed in [`detail`](Self::detail).

#### `prompt`

```ts
prompt: string
```

The prompt's registry name.

#### `variant`

```ts
variant?: string
```

The variant the finding pertains to; `None` (`undefined` in JS) for a prompt-level provenance finding.

## Composition

### `Composition`

```ts
class Composition
```

An explicit, ordered composition of `(Prompt, vars, variant?)` entries that resolves to an ordered `Message[]`. Built with `new Composition()` + `append()` or `Composition.fromMessages([...])`. No fluent `.chain()`. **No Registry**: each entry holds an owned `Prompt` object. `resolve()` takes no arguments â€” it renders using each entry's stored `Prompt` handle directly. No-partial guarantee: if any entry fails validation at `append`/`fromMessages`, the whole call throws and **nothing** is stored. If an entry fails at `resolve` (unknown variant, undefined variable, parse/render), `resolve` throws and the partial result is discarded.

#### `length`

```ts
get length(): number
```

The number of appended entries (== the resolved-message count on success).

#### `append`

```ts
append(entry: CompositionEntry): void
```

Marshal + store one CompositionEntry. When `entry.schema` is present, validation runs here (`schema.safeParse(entry.data)`); on failure a PromptValidationError is thrown and nothing is stored. Returns `void` (not `this`): intentionally **not** fluent.

#### `fromMessages`

```ts
static fromMessages(entries: readonly CompositionEntry[]): Composition
```

Build a composition from an ordered array of CompositionEntry objects, validating each in order. The first entry whose `schema.safeParse(data)` fails throws a PromptValidationError and **no** `Composition` is returned (no partial state â€” ).

#### `resolve`

```ts
resolve(): Message[]
```

Resolve the composition to an ordered `Message[]`, rendering each entry in append order through the kernel (via `NapiPrompt.renderPrompt` on each stored handle). **No Registry** â€” each entry holds its own `NapiPrompt` handle (T046). One entry's failure (unknown variant, undefined variable, parse/render error) throws the mapped PromptingPressError subclass and the partial result is discarded â€” never returned as success. An empty composition resolves to `[]`.

### `CompositionEntry`

```ts
interface CompositionEntry
```

One composition entry, as an **options object**. - `prompt` â€” the `Prompt` object to render. - `schema` â€” optional Zod-like schema. Present â‡’ `schema.safeParse(data)` runs at append. - `data` â€” the vars value (validated against `schema` when present). - `variant` â€” the selected variant arm (absent â‡’ the reserved `default`).

#### `data`

```ts
data: unknown
```

The vars value passed to the prompt renderer (validated against `schema` when present).

#### `prompt`

```ts
prompt: Prompt
```

The `Prompt` object to render.

#### `schema`

```ts
schema?: ZodLikeSchema<unknown>
```

Optional Zod-like schema. When present, `schema.safeParse(data)` runs at append time.

#### `variant`

```ts
variant?: string
```

The selected variant arm. Absent means the reserved `default` variant.

## Message

### `Message`

```ts
interface Message
```

One resolved message in a composition's output: a role-tagged rendered string. The Node mirror of the consumer's `Message` (). `role` is the prompt definition's role stringified (`"system"` / `"user"` / `"assistant"`); `text` is that prompt rendered with the entry's own validated value. Surfaced as a plain JS object `&#123; role, text &#125;`; a message is produced by [`Composition::resolve`], never constructed from JS.

#### `role`

```ts
role: string
```

The conversational role, taken from the prompt definition's `role`.

#### `text`

```ts
text: string
```

The rendered body text for this entry. The guard text is never concatenated here.

## Errors

### `LoadError`

```ts
class LoadError
```

Raised when a document fails to load: malformed YAML/JSON/TOML, or a prompt-definition shape violation. Nothing is partially loaded. Top-level code `"load"`.

### `PromptingPressError`

```ts
class PromptingPressError
```

The base class for every failure the library raises. Carries the normalized, already-scrubbed `errors` rows. Every thrown error is an `instanceof PromptingPressError`; callers branch on the concrete subclass (or on `err.errors[*].code`) and never see a `ZodError` or a native napi error.

#### `errors`

```ts
readonly errors: readonly FieldError[]
```

The structured, scrubbed failure rows (the cross-language contract).

### `PromptRenderError`

```ts
class PromptRenderError
```

Raised when the kernel rejects a render: an unknown variant, a referenced-but-undefined root variable (the loud three-sets gap â€” never a silent empty render), a parse/render failure, or an excluded template feature. Maps the kernel codes `unknown_variant` / `undefined_variable` / `parse` / `render` / `excluded_feature`. `parse`/`render`/`excluded_feature` detail is scrubbed.

### `PromptValidationError`

```ts
class PromptValidationError
```

Raised when typed input fails validation â€” either the facade's Zod `safeParse` (before any templating) or a garde-class validation surfaced by the consumer. Top-level code `"validation"`. It is **not** a `ZodError`: the facade copies only the value-free issue message + path.

### `FieldError`

```ts
interface FieldError
```

One normalized failure row. The TS mirror of the Rust consumer's `FieldError` and the cross-language error contract. Reconstructed by the facade from the addon's JSON payload (or, for a Zod validation failure, built locally from `issue.message` + `issue.path`).

#### `code`

```ts
readonly code: string
```

A stable code from the consumer's closed vocabulary (e.g. `"validation"`, `"render"`).

#### `field`

```ts
readonly field: string
```

The offending field or dotted path; `""` when no single field applies.

#### `message`

```ts
readonly message: string
```

A human-readable, scrubbed message safe to log (never the rejected value).

### `ZodLikeSchema`

```ts
interface ZodLikeSchema
```

The minimal structural contract this facade needs from a Zod schema: a `safeParse` returning a tagged result. Typing it structurally (rather than importing Zod's concrete `ZodType`) keeps the facade decoupled from a specific Zod minor and lets a caller pass any object exposing the same shape â€” the library never depends on Zod's identity, only on `safeParse`. The optional `shape` field supports the `validation_required` coverage check at construction: if present (it is on a `ZodObject`), `field in schema.shape` proves that a `validation_required` variable is covered. If absent, the coverage check is skipped with a documented "cannot assert coverage" limitation.

#### `shape`

```ts
shape?: Record<string, unknown>
```

Optional: `ZodObject.shape` â€” a record of field name â†’ ZodType. Present on a `ZodObject`; absent on other schema types. When absent, `validation_required` coverage cannot be introspected and the check is skipped (documented limitation).

#### `safeParse`

```ts
safeParse(data: unknown): ZodSafeParseResult<T>
```

Validate `data` and return a tagged success/failure result without throwing.

### `coreVersion`

```ts
coreVersion(): string
```

Returns the kernel version, reached through the Rust consumer surface. Retained from the spec-001 stub so the addon exports a trivial callable and the dependency edges onto `prompting-press`/`prompting-press-core` stay load-bearing. Surfaces as `coreVersion` in JS (napi renames snake_case â†’ camelCase).

### `ValidatorMap`

```ts
type ValidatorMap = ZodLikeSchema
```

A validator bound at `Prompt` construction. In practice this is a Zod schema (a `ZodObject`) whose `shape` property the construction-time `validation_required` coverage check introspects. Typed as `ZodLikeSchema` so callers aren't forced to import Zod types.

## Shape types

- [`PromptDefinition`](/prompting-press/reference/prompt-definition/) â€” see the Prompt definition shape.

- [`PromptVariable`](/prompting-press/reference/prompt-definition/) â€” see the Prompt definition shape.

- [`PromptVariant`](/prompting-press/reference/prompt-definition/) â€” see the Prompt definition shape.
